hints.text: US overseas territory — **counts as US** in country streaks
Nearly all coverage = **smallcam** (wide round low blur, antenna often visible); Gen3 car has a **front-right antenna w/ a small white dot on the tip**

shared_with.note: Guam & NMI cars also have front-right antennas.
shared_with.diff: PARE + Spanish → Puerto Rico; Pacific setting → Guam/NMI.

hints.text: Whole island = phone code **(787)** — format (787) XXX-XXXX (rarer: 939)
**US store chains (Wal-Mart etc.) everywhere** — rare in the surrounding Caribbean/LatAm
hints.uniq_note: US chains + Spanish = Puerto Rico's signature combo.

hints.text: **Front plates not mandatory** → front-plated cars rare (differentiates from Guam/NMI)
Much rural coverage on **large divided highways**
Unique **PR highway shields** (primary/urban/secondary/tertiary) — in no other US state or territory
Speed signs = US shape but **always in Spanish**; stop signs say **'PARE'**

shared_with.note: DR also uses PARE (+ all of South America).
shared_with.diff: US signage shapes + smallcam → PR; striped-bar car → DR.

hints.text: km markers: **tall, green, 'km' on top + number written vertically** in large text
Homes of **concrete w/ flat roofs** + metal gates (esp. urban) — similar elsewhere in LatAm
**South = distinctly drier**; north & south coasts = flat plains/rolling hills; middle = mountains (coverage there mostly divided highways)
**Ponce** (south): Ponce-Creole buildings (Victorian × Spanish Revival, wood + masonry); **Old San Juan** (NW tip of San Juan): narrow brick streets, colourful Gothic/Renaissance/Baroque mix
